title: clusia guabalensis
intro: Clusia guabalensis, also known as Guabal or Clusia tree, is a plant species native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Clusiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Clusia guabalensis include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, Clusia guabalensis is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, and it can be found in some traditional medicine practices in Central and South America.
